questions.q: What is the theme of the poem 'Lost Spring'?
opts: (A) Love and heartbreak
(B) Loss of innocence
(C) Passage of time
(D) Nature's beauty
questions.explanation: The poem revolves around the speaker's loss of innocence and the end of carefree childhood days.

questions.q: Who is the speaker in the poem 'Lost Spring'?
opts: (A) A young girl
(B) An old man
(C) A mother
(D) A poet
questions.explanation: The speaker is a young girl who reminisces about her lost childhood and innocence.

questions.q: What is the effect of the use of enjambment in the poem?
opts: (A) It creates a sense of urgency
(B) It slows down the pace of the poem
(C) It emphasizes the speaker's emotions
(D) It disrupts the flow of the poem
questions.explanation: The use of enjambment emphasizes the speaker's emotions and creates a sense of continuity.

questions.q: What is the significance of the title 'Lost Spring'?
opts: (A) It refers to a season
(B) It symbolizes the passing of time
(C) It represents the protagonist's name
(D) It is a metaphor for the speaker's emotions
questions.explanation: The title 'Lost Spring' symbolizes the passing of time and the loss of innocence.

questions.q: How does the speaker's tone change throughout the poem?
opts: (A) It remains consistent throughout
(B) It becomes more optimistic
(C) It becomes more melancholic
(D) It becomes more sarcastic
questions.explanation: The speaker's tone becomes more melancholic as the poem progresses, reflecting the loss of innocence.

opts: (A) They represent the speaker's happiness
(B) They symbolize the passing of time
(C) They represent the speaker's love for nature
(D) They are irrelevant to the poem's theme
